summary:
Jodhpur-Pali-Marwar Industrial Area (JPMIA) in Rajasthan is set to attract ₹7,500 crore ($790 million) in investment and create around 40,000 jobs, with textiles among key sectors. 
Spread over 1,578 acres between Jodhpur and Pali, the project offers strong connectivity and is being developed under the industrial corridor programme, aimed at boosting manufacturing, logistics and regional growth.

title: Vietnam, Japan eye $5 bn yearly investment, $60 bn trade by 2030
summary:
Vietnam and Japan aim to boost ties with $5 billion in annual investment and $60 billion in trade by 2030. 
Leaders signed six agreements, while 2025 saw trade surpass $50 billion alongside rising ODA and investment. 
Both sides plan to deepen cooperation in technology, energy and security, and expand high-tech and supply chain partnerships.

title: South Africa’s textile imports fall in Q1 but rise in March
summary:
South Africa’s textile imports fell 6.8 per cent YoY to 15.6 billion rand in Q1 2026, while exports remained stable.
However, imports rebounded 14.1 per cent in March on a monthly basis.
The country continues to rely on Asian suppliers due to structural challenges, while exports are constrained by high costs and limited value addition capacity.

title: US GDP grows 2% in Q1 2026, driven by investment & exports
summary:
US GDP grew 2 per cent in Q1 2026, accelerating from 0.5 per cent in Q4 2025, driven by investment, exports and government spending. 
Consumer spending slowed, while imports rose. 
Inflation pressures increased, with personal consumption expenditures (PCE) inflation at 4.5 per cent. 
Goods exports were boosted by higher shipments of industrial supplies and materials.

title: China’s industrial profits rise 15.5% in Q1 2026
summary:
China’s industrial profits rose 15.5 per cent YoY to 1.696 trillion yuan (~$234 billion) in Q1 2026, supported by manufacturing growth and cost control. 
Revenues grew 5 per cent, with margins improving. 
Private firms led gains, while utilities declined. 
Textile and apparel segments saw profit contraction. Rising receivables and inventory levels signalled some pressure on cash cycles.

title: Indian PM Modi to inaugurate Mega Textile Park in Warangal on May 10
summary:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the PM MITRA Kakatiya Mega Textile Park in Warangal on May 10. 
The ₹1,695 crore project, spread over 1,327 acres, has attracted over ₹6,000 crore in investments. 
With most infrastructure ready and 62 per cent area allotted, it is expected to generate 24,400 jobs and boost India’s textile manufacturing and exports.

title: ‘We confront the spectre of a global recession’: UN Secretary-General
summary:
UN chief António Guterres warned that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could trigger a global recession, pushing millions into poverty and hunger. 
Even a quick resolution would slow growth and raise inflation. 
Prolonged disruptions may worsen energy shortages, strain supply chains and reverse development gains, highlighting urgent need for stability.

title: Australian wool prices edge higher, quality gap weighs on market
summary:
Australian wool prices edged higher, with EMI up slightly, though US dollar returns weakened and WMI declined. 
Mixed trends were seen across wool types, with crossbreds gaining and Merino types easing. 
A larger offering increased quality variation, leading to discounts on lower-grade wool.
With supply set to fall to 34,290 bales next week, market direction will depend on demand and quality.

title: China’s logistics sector grows 6.2% in Q1 2026
summary:
China’s logistics sector recorded steady growth in Q1 2026, with total social logistics value rising 6.2 per cent year on year (YoY) to ¥96.4 trillion (~$14 trillion).
Industrial activity remained the key driver, while online retail and consumption trends supported demand.
Authorities aim to enhance supply chains and accelerate digital, green transformation.

title: Middle East conflict clouds India’s FY27 GDP forecast of 7-7.4%: Govt
summary:
The Middle East crisis has weighed on India's industrial performance in early FY27, with softening business sentiment and e-way bill growth declining, the department of economic affairs said. 
A ‘supply shock’ is apparent in the economy, it noted. 
Though the crisis has slightly dampened bankers' optimism regarding loan demand and terms for the Q1 FY27, financial instability is not a threat, it said.

title: How recent wage hike put India’s NCR T&A sector to test
summary:
Sharp wage hikes are triggering a cost shock, severely squeezing margins for NCR textile and apparel manufacturers. 
MSMEs are most vulnerable, with rising labour costs threatening financial stability and operations. 
Industry fears loss of competitiveness. 
Unrest and policy uncertainty risk damaging NCR’s credibility as a reliable manufacturing hub.

title: Brazil’s apparel imports double in 5 years, momentum persists
summary:
Brazil’s apparel imports have more than doubled in five years, with 2026 maintaining strong momentum.
Q1 alone accounts for nearly one-third of 2025’s total, signalling potential record highs.
Growth is driven by resilient demand and rising import dependence, with a clear shift towards affordable, high-volume basics like T-shirts over premium segments.

title: US’ Crocs' Q1 strong on DTC growth; margins, EPS decline
summary:
Crocs has reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 results, with revenue at $921 million, down 1.7 per cent, driven by 12.1 per cent DTC growth.  Gross margin fell to 56.8 per cent, while EPS dipped to $2.71. 
The Crocs brand grew modestly, but HEYDUDE declined. 
CEO Andrew Rees highlighted strong consumer demand and raised FY26 guidance, projecting EPS of $13.20-13.75.

title: US' Columbia ups FY26 outlook on tariff relief; Q1 profit dips
summary:
Columbia Sportswear Company has raised its FY26 outlook, projecting $3.43-3.5 billion in sales and EPS of $3.55-4, aided by tariff relief. 
Q1 sales were flat at $779 million, with profit declining due to US weakness and tariffs. 
International growth remained strong. 
The company expects wholesale recovery in H2. 
Q2 sales are seen at $600-610 million, with a wider operating loss anticipated.

title: India aligns RoDTEP schedules with amended customs tariff
summary:
India has aligned RoDTEP schedules with the amended customs tariff structure, effective May 1, 2026.
The revision updates Appendix 4R and 4RE, impacting 194 tariff lines with additions, deletions and modifications.
It aims to ensure seamless system integration, reduce classification ambiguity and improve export processing.
The move ensures continuity in duty remission benefits.

title: Germany's Puma margins rise despite weak sales in Q1 FY26
summary:
Puma has reported a 1 per cent decline in Q1 FY26 revenue to €1,863.8 million (~$2.18 billion), with a sharper 6.3 per cent drop on a reported basis due to currency headwinds. 
Gross margin improved to 47.7 per cent, supported by cost efficiencies and inventory clearance. 
The company reaffirmed its FY26 outlook, expecting sales pressure and negative EBIT as it continues restructuring.

title: Imposing Section 301 tariffs would be counterproductive: FLWG to USTR
summary:
The US Forced Labor Working Group has urged the USTR not to impose Section 301 tariffs, as these would be counterproductive, legally unwarranted, and harmful to commerce. 
If this probe leads to tariff actions, good actors that have invested in forced-labor compliance would face higher costs, with no benefit, while bad actors would gain by sourcing from lower-cost alternatives in regions with less oversight

title: Canada’s Gildan's Q1 strong on HanesBrands integration, outlook steady
summary:
Gildan has reported a strong Q1 FY26, driven by HanesBrands integration, and reaffirmed full-year guidance. 
Net sales rose sharply, while margins were impacted by acquisition-related costs. 
Retail sales surged, offsetting weaker wholesale performance. 
Adjusted margins exceeded guidance, supported by pricing actions. 
The company remains focused on operational efficiency and cost discipline.

title: Frasers Group acquires two major UK designer outlets
summary:
Frasers Group plc has acquired York Designer Outlet and East Midlands Designer Outlet, adding over 400,000 sq. ft of retail space across 185+ brands. 
With a combined 7.8 million annual footfall, the move strengthens its dual role as landlord and retailer, supports global outlet strategies, and accelerates its Elevation Strategy to grow UK market share and brand ecosystem.

title: AAFA pushes for swift US House passage of key anti-counterfeiting law
summary:
The American Apparel & Footwear Association has urged the US House of Representatives to pass a key anti-counterfeiting measure that is scheduled to be considered this week. 
The legislation (HR 4930) aims at strengthening US Customs and Border Protection’s ability to share information with stakeholders during enforcement of American intellectual property rights at the border.
